column1: # NASA Exoplanet Archive template for reporting planet parameters for confirmed or validated planets detected by transit and/or radial velocity
# This template was prepared by: [e.g. EXOFASTv2
# * indicates a required row; other rows can be removed or commented out as necessary
# Each file represents one gravitationally-bound system (which can include multiple stars and planets)
# Each section has example rows (commented out) for guidance; they can be removed or left commented out
# All parameters under a 'st_name' or 'pl_name' row are considered to belong to the named object
column2: until a different name is reached (or the end of the file).

column1: # Users are expected to take note of the units anticipated by this template
# If the system has more than one star
column2: copy and paste the stellar parameters section multiple times

column1: # just be sure to set the host_star_flag to 1 for the star/s that host the planets (two stars can have host_star_flag = 1 for circumbinary planets)
# Names for planets provided in this template must adhere to the accepted catalogs in the NASA Exoplanet Archive (see link below)
# Planets requiring new catalog names to be defined (i.e. the first confirmed planet from a new survey) cannot be submitted using this template
# Uncertainties are assumed to be 1-sigma uncertainties
# No specific planet parameter is required but at least one planet parameter must be specified
# Planet parameters must be published in an accepted and refereed journal to be submitted with this template

column3: and precisions provided in this file MUST match the values
column5: and precisions published in the accepted paper

column1: # Stellar and planet parameters delivered in this file MUST appear in the accepted paper; do not include additional parameters here which are not in the paper
# Please submit a helpdesk ticket for assistance with this template: https://exoplanetarchive.ipac.caltech.edu/cgi-bin/Helpdesk/nph-genTicketForm

column2: Optional additional alias if confirmed by multiple projects [e.g. MASCARA-2 b]
*Planetary Parameter Reference [arxiv link or ADS bibcode
column3: paper must be accepted in a refereed journal]

column2: Equatorial Planet Radius [Earth Radius] [Note: will be converted to units of Jupiter Radius unless pl_radj supplied]
Equatorial Planet Radius Upper Unc. [Earth Radius]
Equatorial Planet Radius Lower Unc. [Earth Radius]
Equatorial Planet Radius [Jupiter Radius] [Note: will be converted to units of Earth Radius unless pl_rade supplied]
Equatorial Planet Radius Upper Unc. [Jupiter Radius]
Equatorial Planet Radius Lower Unc. [Jupiter Radius]
Planet Mass [Earth Mass] [Note: will be converted to units of Jupiter Mass unless pl_massj or pl_msinij supplied]
Planet Mass [Jupiter Mass] [Note: will be converted to units of Earth mass unless pl_masse or pl_msine supplied]
Planet Mass*sin(i) [Earth Mass] [Note: will be converted to units of Jupiter Mass unless pl_massj or pl_msinij supplied]
Planet Mass*sin(i) [Jupiter Mass] [Note: will be converted to units of Earth Mass unless pl_masse or pl_msinie supplied]

column4: or Time of Minimum Projected Separation [days]

column2: *Host star of planets in this file [1 = host star
column3: 0 = non-host star in system; if two stars have hostflag=1
column4: the planet is assumed to be circumbinary]

column2: *Stellar Parameter Reference [arxiv link or ADS bibcode] [Note: you can refer to a previously published set of stellar parameters here without repeating them below]

column1: # Note: All flags are assumed 0 by default
column2: if not set to 1 in this section
Radial Velocity Detection Flag [1 = planet host star exhibits measured radial velocity variations due to the planet]
Transit Detection Flag [1 = planet is observed to transit its host star]
Astrometry Detection Flag [1 = planet host star is observed to exhibit astrometric variations due to the planet]
Imaging Detection Flag [1 = planet has been observed via the imaging technique]
Orbital Brightness Modulation Detection Flag [1 = orbital phase curve of planet is observed to have brightness modulations caused by the planet]
Data show Transit Timing Variations induced by another planet in the system [1 = transit times of the planet are observed to vary]
Data show Eclipse Timing Variations [1 = eclipse times of circumbinary host stars are observed to vary due to the planet]
